task: I need a short client-facing update for a network issue that’s already been narrowed down, and I want it to read cleanly without extra filler. Please draft something I could send after a quick review.

turns.agent_response: Next step: we’re validating the affected network segment and confirming the fix path. The issue has been narrowed to the local network path serving the impacted service. We’ve ruled out the upstream carrier and the core network as the source.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but make it tighter and more client-facing. Don’t say "we’re validating" unless it’s actually in progress — just give the status and next action. Something like:

"We’ve narrowed the issue to the local network path serving the impacted service. Upstream carrier and core network have been ruled out. We’re now confirming the exact fault point and the fix path."

If you want, I can make it sound a little more polished or a little more hard-line depending on the client.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a tighter client-facing version:

“We’ve narrowed the issue to the local network path serving the impacted service. The upstream carrier and core network have been ruled out. We’re now confirming the exact fault point and the fix path.”

If you want, I can make it a bit more polished or a bit more firm depending on the client.

user_simulator.text: Yeah. That works. Clean, client-facing, no extra fluff.
